There are three preferred antiviral drugs available for the initial treatment of shingles: Acyclovir Valacyclovir Famciclovir These antivirals accelerate the resolution of lesions; reduce the development of new lesions and viral shedding; and decrease the severity of acute pain. Randomized trials have indicated that three FDA-approved antiviral medications provide clinical benefit for genital herpes: acyclovir, valacyclovir, and famciclovir ( 463 – 471 ). claims.short_snippet: escribing NICE guideline Reference number: NG84 Published: 26 January 2018 Download guidance (PDF) Overview This guideline sets out an antimicrobial prescribing strategy for acute sore throat. It aims to limit antibiotic use and reduce antimicrobial resistance. Acute sore throat is often caused by a virus, lasts for about a week, and most people get better without antibiotics.
